name: El Ateneo Grand Splendid
description: This historic, palatial theater is now one of the world’s most beautiful bookstores.

name: Perito Moreno Glacier
description: This stunning mass of ice spans 121 square miles and is still growing.

name: Iguazu Falls
description: A series of waterfalls so massive they straddle the border of Argentina and Brazil.

name: La Manzana de las Luces
description: A gateway to a mysterious network of underground tunnels.

name: Xul Solar Museum
description: Xul Solar was an artist of alternate worlds, inventor of languages, and dreamer of utopias.

name: La Cueva de las Manos (Cave of Hands)
description: Stenciled handprints and wall paintings dating back 10,000 years, some of the earliest forms of cave art.

name: Cementerio de la Recoleta
description: Buenos Aires' city of the dead contains some very famous remains, including Eva Perón.

name: Caminito
description: A bright little alley in La Boca is a part of tango history.

name: Palacio Barolo
description: A tower devoted to — and modeled after — the Divine Comedy.

name: El Zanjón de Granados
description: Museum housed in underground tunnels that date back to Buenos Aires's earliest settlements.

name: Cerro Fitz Roy
description: The inspiration for the Patagonia Clothing logo, Cerro Fitz Roy turns pink at sunrise.

name: Campanópolis
description: An Argentinian businessman facing terminal cancer built this whimsical medieval village using salvaged materials.

name: Floralis Generica
description: 105-foot wide giant metallic flower blooms anew every day in the heart of Buenos Aires.

name: Villa Epecuen
description: Once swallowed by flood waters, this village has emerged as an eerie shadow of its former self.

name: Laguna del Diamante
description: A toxic lake near a volcanic caldera that is home to flamingos and other life.

name: Pasaje Lanín
description: A vibrant street away from the typical tourist track, lined with colorful, mosaic-covered houses.

name: Ischigualasto
description: Massive rock sculptures formed during the time of the dinosaurs still stand undisturbed.

name: The Tomb of Rufina Cambacérès
description: A beautiful young girl said to have been buried alive in her coffin…

name: Fuente de las Nereidas
description: This beautiful fountain was sculpted by a female artist who was ahead of her time.

name: Castillo de Naveira
description: A neo-Gothic castle in the Argentinian countryside.

name: Bahia Lapataia at the End of the World
description: The most southern point you can drive to in the Western Hemisphere.

name: Strange House
description: Visit this odd display of indigenous style in Argentine wine country.

name: Glaciarium
description: This glacier interpretation center houses the world’s only bar made completely from glacial ice.

name: Coloso
description: This giant power line tower is a robot that can express its moods in neon.

name: Puente del Inca
description: A brightly colored natural bridge formed by mineral-rich hot springs high up in the Andes.

name: Cementerio de la Chacarita
description: A vast necropolis where you'll find the elaborate mausoleums of Argentinian tango stars.

name: Casa Redonda
description: A mysterious architectural gem in one of the most elegant areas of Buenos Aires.

name: Thermal Baths of Villavicencio
description: Mineral springs believed to hold therapeutic properties draw in visitors from all over.

name: Parque Rivadavia Book Market
description: With its vast selection of books, comics, and records, this nearly 70-year-old open-air market always rewards the patient browser.

name: La Casa Mínima
description: The narrowest house in Buenos Aires is less than 10 feet wide.

name: Mafalda Monument
description: These small sculptures pay homage to the lovable star of an iconic Argentine comic strip.

name: Ricardo Rojas House Museum
description: A pocket of serenity in hectic Buenos Aires, complete with a peaceful courtyard and massive library.

name: Ovnipuerto Cachi
description: A Swiss man built this airstrip for aliens because a UFO captain telepathically told him to.

name: Wanda Mines
description: A small but industrious mining operation that allows visitors to touch precious gems while they are still in the rock.

name: Hotel Edén
description: This magnificent abandoned hotel has long been haunted by its Nazi past.

name: Tafí del Valle Menhires
description: Mysterious and ancient carved standing stones.

name: El Viejo Hobbit
description: "Di amigo y entra" at this Argentinian Middle Earth-inspired pub.

name: Butch Cassidy's Cabin
description: With the Wild Bunch hunted almost to extinction and the law closing in on him, Butch Cassidy fled to this remote cabin in Argentina.

name: Ecological Plastic Bottle House
description: One family's mission to exemplify sustainability took on a whole new life as a surprisingly beautiful home.

name: Tierra Santa
description: Bible stories are brought to life with gloriously cartoonish kitsch in this South American theme park.

name: Borges Memorial Maze
description: This Argentinian hedge maze is an intricate tribute to a famous surrealist author.

name: Petrified Forest of José de Ormachea
description: An ancient forest of the Paleocene Age turned to stone.

name: Jaramillo Petrified Forest
description: Conifers from the Jurassic Period transformed into stone in Patagonia.

name: Rocsen Museum
description: One man's quest to collect artifacts from every aspect of human life has resulted in this eclectic Argentinian museum.

name: Abandoned Cemetery of San Andres de Giles
description: Crumbling crypts and overgrown graves rise just out of arm's reach in this forgotten Buenos Aires graveyard.

name: Parque de la Memoria - Memory Park
description: A memorial to the victims of Argentina's "Dirty War."

name: Villa Ocampo
description: A beautiful riverside mansion where the intellectual life of early-20th-century Argentina flourished.

name: Sarmiento House
description: The house of a former Argentinian president stands protected within a large glass case.

name: Argentine Vintage Funeral Cadillacs
description: The clients of these mysterious hand-crafted funeral cars took their last ride in style.

name: Mysterious Ruins
description: Are they the remains of a fallen hotel, or the final stronghold for escaped Nazis?

name: The Time Capsule of Ushuaia
description: This plaza at the end of the world teems with monumental tributes to a problematic history, and laser discs.

name: Campo del Cielo Meteorite Field
description: A vast landscape of craters created by meteorites.

name: Original Tomb of Tomás Guido
description: The humble tomb of an Argentine general, hand-built by his son using stones brought down from the Andes.

name: Villa Freud
description: A pocket of Buenos Aires with an unusually high concentration of psychotherapists.

name: Pedro Martín Ureta's Forest Guitar
description: Guitar made of trees, dedicated to a man's lost love.

name: Mausoleum of José de San Martín
description: The final resting place of the liberator of Argentina is guarded around the clock by two soldiers.

name: La Piedra Movediza (Shifting Stone)
description: A replica of a beloved, delicately-balanced granite boulder.

name: Rueda Eiffel
description: An intriguing antique iron Ferris wheel that most locals are sure was designed by Gustave Eiffel.

name: Casa de Botellas 'La Fortaleza'
description: An artist's statement of new uses for ordinary and discarded materials.

name: Azul Cemetery Entrance
description: This colossal structure and its accompanying angel often play tricks on the eyes.

name: Sanctuaries of Difunta Correa
description: Trash-filled shrines to an unofficial saint known for her endless breast milk dot the highways of Argentina.

name: The House of Ernesto Sábato
description: Honor one of Argentina's most prolific writers at his historic home.

name: Plaza Primero de Mayo
description: You have to know where to look in this rather ordinary-looking square to find hints of its past as a dissidents' cemetery.

name: Llao Llao Resort
description: One of South America's most exclusive resorts has a storied history dating back to World War II.

name: Las Bóvedas de Uspallata (The Vaults of Uspallata)
description: These egg-shaped buildings constructed by Jesuits house a quaint museum in the Andes.

name: Lourdes' Grotto
description: A grotto replicating one of the world's most famed Marian apparitions sits across the ocean from the original.

name: Postal del Fin del Mundo
description: The southernmost postal office in South America.

name: Cementerio del Andinista
description: A cemetery dedicated to the mountaineers who perished climbing Mount Aconcagua.

name: Historic Tram of Buenos Aires
description: One of the few remnants of an extensive tram network that once made Buenos Aires the “City of Trams.”

name: Casa Auto
description: A father built this one-of-a-kind house in the shape of his son's race car.

name: El Bosque Tallado
description: This mountainside open-air museum was built from the ashes left behind by a devastating fire.

name: Wreck of the Marjory Glen
description: This rusty hull was used as fighter plane target practice during the Falklands War.

name: La Payunia Reserve
description: An otherworldly reserve of one of Earth’s densest volcanic fields and the world’s largest guanaco migration.

name: Pasaje Pan
description: This 19th-century shopping arcade has charming, unusual stores like a luthier workshop and even the local Esperanto Association.

name: Cabildo de Córdoba
description: One of the oldest colonial buildings still standing in South America.

name: Shrine to Gauchito Gil
description: This roadside memorial pays homage to Argentina's Outlaw Saint.

name: Museo Porteño 'Lo de Gustavo'
description: A medley of objects that represent Argentine culture, from cars to toys.

name: Costanera Sur Ecological Reserve
description: From a 1930s bathing beach to a 1970s dumping ground, this stretch of Buenos Aires is now its largest and wildest reserve.

name: Casa Barco Irupé
description: A poet in love with the Parana River built this unique house that became a landmark.

name: El Desdemona Shipwreck
description: A remote shipwreck at the end of the world.

name: Argentina's Miniature Statue of Liberty
description: The Estatua de la Libertad stands discretely on an obscure tangent path in Barrancas de Belgrano.

name: Mausoleum of Nestor Kirchner
description: An imposing mausoleum that houses the remains of the former Argentinian president.

name: Molino García (Garcia Mill)
description: A perfectly preserved 19th-century mill that can still run today.

name: Huemul Island
description: The island houses the ruins of a secret nuclear fusion lab that once employed high-profile Nazi scientists

name: Parque el Desafío
description: A retiree's challenge to build an outdoor wonderland out of trash.
